2605.02363.title: When Correct Isn't Usable: Improving Structured Output Reliability in Small Language Models
2605.02363.paper_summary: Deployed language models must produce outputs that are both correct and format-compliant. We study this structured-output reliability gap using two mathematical benchmarks -- GSM8K and MATH -- as a controlled testbed: ground truth is unambiguous and the output contract is strict (JSON with required fields). We evaluate three 7-9B models under five prompting strategies and report output accuracy -- the joint event of mathematical correctness and valid JSON structure -- as the primary metric. A systematic format failure emerges: NAIVE prompting (no system prompt) achieves up to 85% task accuracy on GSM8K but 0% output accuracy across all models and datasets. REFERENCE prompting (a minimal hand-written JSON format prompt) fares little better, yielding 0% output accuracy for two of four models tested. Constrained decoding enforces syntactic validity but incurs 3.6x-8.2x latency overhead and in several settings degrades task performance substantially. To overcome this limitation, we developed AloLab, an iterative system-prompt optimizer (meta-agent: Claude Sonnet 4.5) requiring only black-box API access to the target model; it reaches 84-87% output accuracy on GSM8K and 34-40% on MATH across five independent runs per model, with 29/30 paired McNemar comparisons against the best static prompt significant at p < 0.05, at near-NAIVE inference latency and without model fine-tuning. The same format failure extends to GPT-4o (OpenAI, 2024), a proprietary closed-source model: REFERENCE achieves 0% output accuracy due to systematic markdown-fence wrapping, while AloLab reaches 95.2% [94.8, 95.6]. An ablation replacing the Sonnet 4.5 meta-agent with Claude 3 Haiku reduces mean output accuracy to 61.0% and increases run-to-run standard deviation from <1 pp to 21.8 pp, confirming that meta-agent capability is a primary driver of optimization quality.
2605.02363.paper_summary_zh: 部署的語言模型必須產生既正確又符合格式的輸出。<lb/>我們使用兩個數學基準——GSM8K 和 MATH——作為受控測試平台來研究這一結構化輸出可靠性差距：真實情況是明確的，輸出契約是嚴格的（JSON 需包含必要字段）。<lb/>我們在五種提示策略下評估三個 7-9B 模型，並將輸出準確性——數學正確性和有效 JSON 結構的聯合事件——作為主要指標。<lb/>一個系統性的格式失敗出現了：NAIVE 提示（無系統提示）在 GSM8K 上的任務準確性達到 85%，但在所有模型和數據集上的輸出準確性為 0%。<lb/>REFERENCE 提示（最小手寫 JSON 格式提示）的表現稍好，但在測試的四個模型中有兩個的輸出準確性為 0%。<lb/>約束解碼強制執行語法有效性，但會產生 3.6 倍至 8.2 倍的延遲開銷，並在幾種情況下顯著降低任務性能。<lb/>為了克服這一限制，我們開發了 AloLab，一個迭代的系統提示優化器（元代理：Claude Sonnet 4.5），只需對目標模型進行黑箱 API 訪問；它在 GSM8K 上達到 84-87% 的輸出準確性，在 MATH 上達到 34-40%，每個模型進行五次獨立運行，與最佳靜態提示的 29/30 對比 McNemar 在 p < 0.05 下顯著，且在接近 NAIVE 推理延遲的情況下，沒有對模型進行微調。<lb/>同樣的格式失敗也延伸到 GPT-4o（OpenAI，2024），這是一個專有的閉源模型：REFERENCE 由於系統性的 markdown-fence 包裝而達到 0% 的輸出準確性，而 AloLab 則達到 95.2% [94.8, 95.6]。<lb/>一個將 Sonnet 4.5 元代理替換為 Claude 3 Haiku 的消融實驗將平均輸出準確性降低到 61.0%，並將運行間標準差從 <1 pp 增加到 21.8 pp，確認元代理能力是優化質量的主要驅動因素。
2605.02363.author: Cosimo Galeone et.al.
2605.02363.authors: Cosimo Galeone, Minsu Park, Giuseppe Ettorre, Daniele Ligorio
